unit.name: From deep implicits to NeRF and 3D Gaussian splatting
unit.description: Builds from deep implicit geometry to neural radiance fields, differentiable volume rendering, image-only supervision, and the efficiency gains of Gaussian splatting.
unit.summary: Starting from deep occupancy and deep SDF ideas, Wu generalizes neural implicit functions so a network can answer richer queries than inside/outside, including signed distance, density, color, and radiance [ts=3469s][ts=3504s]. NeRF is introduced as the step that pairs these queryable fields with viewing direction and differentiable volume rendering: the network maps spatial position and camera direction to density and color, and the rendering equation is differentiable enough to learn directly from 2D images rather than requiring 3D supervision [ts=3548s][ts=3561s][ts=3571s][ts=3608s][ts=3627s]. Wu then notes that radiance-field ideas can also be embedded in generative pipelines to obtain more realistic controllable 3D outputs from image supervision [ts=3761s][ts=3800s]. The main weakness of NeRF is efficiency, since rays still sample many empty locations; 3D Gaussian splatting addresses this by representing the scene with sparse Gaussian blobs so computation focuses on occupied regions, giving similar rendering quality but dramatically higher frame rates [ts=3842s][ts=3913s][ts=3958s][ts=3994s][ts=4000s].

key_points.text: Deep implicit methods can predict occupancy, signed distance, density, and appearance for queried 3D points.
NeRF queries a neural field with x, y, z and viewing direction to output density and radiance.
Differentiable volume rendering lets NeRF learn from 2D images by accumulating color and transmittance along rays.
Radiance-field generators can provide controllable 3D generation while training directly from images.
3D Gaussian splatting uses sparse Gaussian blobs so rays sample mainly where matter exists.
Gaussian splatting achieves NeRF-like quality with much higher rendering speed.

kp_catalog.name: Implicit functions for geometry
kp_catalog.description: A representation in which surface points satisfy a condition like f(x,y,z)=0, enabling inside/outside reasoning and later neural field methods.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because implicit functions for geometry formalizes how a representation in which surface points satisfy a condition like f(x,y,z)=0, enabling inside/outside reasoning and later neural field methods, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Neural radiance fields and differentiable volume rendering
kp_catalog.description: A neural field that predicts density and color from spatial position and view direction, trained through differentiable rendering from 2D images.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because neural radiance fields and differentiable volume rendering synthesizes a neural field that predicts density and color from spatial position and view direction, trained through differentiable rendering from 2D images into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.

kp_catalog.name: 3D Gaussian splatting
kp_catalog.description: A sparse radiance-field-style representation that stores extended Gaussian blobs in space to avoid repeatedly sampling empty regions.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because 3D Gaussian splatting clarifies a sparse radiance-field-style representation that stores extended Gaussian blobs in space to avoid repeatedly sampling empty regions, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
